pattern: Sort the positions and slide a window that must end up holding all stones; the window with the most stones already inside fixes the minimum, while the gaps at either end bound the maximum.
hints: After sorting, the final configuration is some run of length n with no gaps; you want to land on the window that already contains the most stones.
Slide a window of value-width n over the sorted positions and count how many stones fall inside; the leftover stones are roughly how many you must move.

Maximum moves come from filling the empty slots on one side: max(positions[-1]-positions[1], positions[-2]-positions[0]) - (n-2). For the minimum, watch the special almost-consecutive window where the only gap is at an endpoint, which costs two moves not one.

edge_inputs.description: almost consecutive with a single endpoint gap needs two min moves

stress.description: random distinct positions over a moderate range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, size)
    hi = max(n + 5, n * 3)
    stones = rng.sample(range(1, hi + 1), n)
    return [stones]
stress.note: distinct positions guaranteed; small enough for the oracle
